title: Geraniospermum velutinum
taxonomy: Geraniospermum velutinum is a genus in the family Menispermaceae. It belongs to the order Ranunculales, a group of flowering plants. The species is native to Southeast Asia and is part of the broader angiosperm clade. Geraniospermum velutinum is also classified as a flowering plant within the clade Magnoliophyta. The classification of Geraniospermum velutinum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.

Its leaves and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate fever, colds, and respiratory issues. In addition, Geraniospermum velutinum is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant is also used in traditional remedies for its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties.
